name: Thermite Evidence
description: The 'Thermite Evidence' theory, primarily associated with the 9/11 attacks, posits that controlled demolitions using thermite or thermate explosives played a significant role in the collapse of the World Trade Center towers. Proponents argue that the official explanation of structural failure due to fire and impact alone is insufficient to account for the speed and nature of the collapses. The theory gained traction with the publication of scientific papers claiming the discovery of nano-thermite residue in dust samples from the WTC site. These papers argued that the presence of such materials is indicative of controlled demolition. Mainstream scientific and engineering communities, including the National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST), refute these claims. NIST's investigation concluded that the collapses were primarily caused by structural damage from the impact of the planes and the subsequent fires, weakening the steel support structures. They attribute the alleged 'thermite' residue to other sources, such as paint primers or manufacturing byproducts. Critiques also point to the lack of widespread audible explosions and the impracticality of secretly planting and detonating large quantities of thermite in the buildings. Further, mainstream scientists have questioned the methodology and interpretation of the studies claiming nano-thermite detection, citing potential contamination and misidentification of materials.
subtitle: Claims of explosives in WTC collapses

claims.claim: Nano-thermite was found in WTC dust
claims.evidence: Peer-reviewed papers published in scientific journals claiming the identification of nano-thermite residues.
claims.criticism: NIST and other experts argue that the materials found are likely from other sources, such as paint primers or manufacturing byproducts, and not from thermite explosives. Questionable methodology and potential contamination are also cited.

claims.claim: The speed of the collapse was too fast for gravity alone
claims.evidence: Analysis of videos showing the towers collapsing at near free-fall speed, suggesting controlled demolition.
claims.criticism: NIST's simulations and explanations detail how the sequential failure of weakened support columns led to a progressive collapse that accelerated as it proceeded.

claims.claim: Molten metal was present under the WTC debris
claims.evidence: Eyewitness accounts and photographic evidence of molten metal at the base of the WTC site weeks after the collapses.
claims.criticism: NIST attributes this to the prolonged burning of organic materials within the debris pile, as well as reactions between aluminum and water. This explanation is widely accepted within the scientific community as the most plausible cause.

keyFigures.name: Steven E. Jones
keyFigures.role: Physicist and leading proponent of the thermite theory.

keyFigures.name: NIST (National Institute of Standards and Technology)
keyFigures.role: Conducted the official investigation and refuted the thermite claims.

mythologyConnections: The Phoenix (symbolic rebirth from ashes, twisted to represent demolition and rebuilding)
Icarus (hubris leading to a fall, mirroring the collapse of the towers)

timeline.event: The World Trade Center towers collapse.
Papers published claiming the discovery of nano-thermite in WTC dust.
NIST releases its final reports on the collapse of the WTC towers.

sources.author: Jones, Steven E. et al.
